customer_anxiety_profile: Low expressed anxiety but low conviction — Daniel agreed to the appointment without being sold on it. His 'good enough' exit and five-competing-calls comment suggest he views this as one of several parallel evaluations, not a prioritized decision.
unresolved_concerns: Competitive saturation: Daniel has received five calls from competing companies and has not been given a reason to prioritize Ion's appointment over the others.
Appointment format ambiguity: Ella framed this as an 'in person appointment' — if it is actually a phone call, Daniel's expectations need to be reset at the start of the closer call.
sensitive_topics_raised: State incentives for 'South Wales United' utility — Daniel asked specifically and was told the specialist would have the answer. Closer must be prepared to address this.
exact_promises_rep_made: Appointment booked for Saturday April 25 at 10:00AM.
Closer will cover: estimate, design, warranty, programs, and state incentives.
Confirmation text will be sent with appointment details.
A confirmation text (yes/no) will be sent the day before or day of the appointment.
closer_should_know: Daniel has five competing companies in play and closed with 'good enough' — he is not convinced Ion is worth prioritizing. The closer should open by acknowledging the competitive context and immediately differentiating Ion before moving into the proposal, rather than assuming the appointment itself signals buy-in.
